meta.title: Veneration vs. Adoration: Eastern Orthodox Iconology and Red Herrings
meta.author: Pastor Joshua Schooping

questions.question: According to the lecture, why is the adoration-versus-veneration distinction called a "red herring" in much Orthodox icon apologetics?
options: Because it distracts from the claim that refusing icon veneration is treated as a salvation issue
Because the Seventh Council explicitly forbids any bowing before images
Because John of Damascus rejected all use of syllogisms in theology
Because Eastern Orthodoxy officially denies conciliar authority
questions.answer: Because it distracts from the claim that refusing icon veneration is treated as a salvation issue

questions.question: How does the speaker characterize the Seventh Ecumenical Council's stance on venerating icons?
options: A matter of private devotion left to each believer's conscience
A matter of necessity under anathema, not mere permissibility
A disciplinary rule that can be set aside by economia
A recommendation that became obsolete after the Reformation
questions.answer: A matter of necessity under anathema, not mere permissibility

questions.question: Which collection does the speaker cite for Seventh Ecumenical Council documents (including the page references he gives)?
options: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Series I
Schaff's History of the Christian Church, volume 3 only
Nicene and Post-Nicene Fathers, Second Series, Volume 14
The Rudder (Pedalion) in English, 19th-century edition only
questions.answer: Nicene and Post-Nicene Fathers, Second Series, Volume 14

questions.question: In the council's explanation quoted in the lecture, what twofold signification does the Greek root the speaker vocalizes as "kinin" (in his discussion of proskynēsis) carry?
options: To anathematize and to excommunicate
To baptize and to chrismate
To confess and to commune
To salute or venerate and to kiss
questions.answer: To salute or venerate and to kiss

questions.question: According to the lecture, economia is said to apply to which kind of church matters?
options: Disciplinary situations (for example, length of excommunication), not the heart of dogmatic canons
Revising dogmatic definitions pronounced by ecumenical councils
Removing anathemas from all past conciliar statements
Replacing icon veneration with mental prayer alone
questions.answer: Disciplinary situations (for example, length of excommunication), not the heart of dogmatic canons

questions.question: On page 531 (as cited), how does the lecture quote the council's description of the anathema of the whole Catholic Church?
options: As a temporary suspension from receiving the Eucharist
As consigning one to outer darkness prepared for the devil and his angels
As a request for pastoral clarification
As identical to a bishop's private blessing
questions.answer: As consigning one to outer darkness prepared for the devil and his angels

questions.question: What definition of anathema does the lecture quote from the council's treatment of those who debate icon veneration further?
options: A mild warning that does not affect salvation
Complete separation from the visible church only, with salvation still possible
Nothing less than complete separation from God
A synonym for economia in disciplinary cases
questions.answer: Nothing less than complete separation from God

questions.question: The Orthodox canon-law handbook the speaker cites on anathema (page 49) is published by which press?
options: Oxford University Press
Penguin Classics
Eerdmans Publishing
Holy Cross Orthodox Press
questions.answer: Holy Cross Orthodox Press

questions.question: From which book does the lecture quote C. F. W. Walther on wrongly dividing Law and Gospel?
options: The Proper Distinction Between Law and Gospel
The Bondage of the Will
Institutes of the Christian Religion
Christian Dogmatics
questions.answer: The Proper Distinction Between Law and Gospel

questions.question: The Walther quotation used in the lecture comes from which dated setting, according to the speaker?
options: The Formula of Concord, 1577
His 32nd evening lecture on June 19, 1885
The Missouri Synod founding convention in 1847
The Augsburg Confession presentation in 1530
questions.answer: His 32nd evening lecture on June 19, 1885

questions.question: In Revelation 19:9-10 (as discussed), what does the angel tell John after John falls at his feet to worship?
options: That veneration is acceptable because the angel is not God
That John should venerate the angel but not God
That John must not do it—worship God
That proskynēsis toward angels is required for salvation
questions.answer: That John must not do it—worship God

questions.question: In Acts 10:25-26 (as discussed), how does Peter respond when Cornelius meets him and worships?
options: He accepts the worship as appropriate to an apostle
He tells Cornelius to worship only after baptism
He asks Cornelius to bring an icon instead
He lifts him up and says he too is a man—do not venerate him
questions.answer: He lifts him up and says he too is a man—do not venerate him

questions.question: What point does the speaker make about those who tried to venerate the angel (Revelation) or Peter (Acts)?
options: They were clearly confusing the creature with God the Creator
They were corrected only for using the wrong Greek verb
They were idolaters in the sense of Lystra (Paul mistaken for Hermes)
They were not treating the angel or Peter as God; the refusal was not merely fixing latria confusion
questions.answer: They were not treating the angel or Peter as God; the refusal was not merely fixing latria confusion

questions.question: Besides Christ and Mary, which groups' images does the lecture say the Seventh Council's material includes in the scope of honorable images?
options: Only Roman emperors and civic patrons
Angels, saints, and pious people (among others named in the citations)
Only abstract symbols without human figures
Only the cross, with no human images
questions.answer: Angels, saints, and pious people (among others named in the citations)

questions.question: When addressing the claim that Orthodoxy does not anathematize those outside the church, what historical example does the speaker name?
options: The Council of Trent
The Synod of Dort
The Synod of Orthodoxy
The Westminster Assembly
questions.answer: The Synod of Orthodoxy

questions.question: John of Damascus is mentioned in the lecture chiefly in connection with which issues (as summarized)?
options: Denying all use of images in Christian worship
His incomplete scriptural argument, invalid syllogism, and positive view of syllogisms in theology
Rejecting the Seventh Ecumenical Council outright
Teaching that icons are optional for salvation
questions.answer: His incomplete scriptural argument, invalid syllogism, and positive view of syllogisms in theology

questions.question: Which New Testament passage does the lecture cite where Paul warns against being disqualified by asceticism and "worship of angels" (threskeia)?
options: Colossians (applied by the speaker to critique ascetic and visionary piety tied to icon veneration)
Romans 8
Hebrews 1
1 Corinthians 8
questions.answer: Colossians (applied by the speaker to critique ascetic and visionary piety tied to icon veneration)

questions.question: What phrase does the speaker use for tying mandatory veneration of images directly to salvation?
options: The covenant of works
The Eastern Orthodox false gospel of veneration
Theosis by icons alone
The invisible church of images
questions.answer: The Eastern Orthodox false gospel of veneration

questions.question: According to the lecture's report of the council's reasoning, English "worship" as a translation of proskynēsis implies what?
options: Only interior thoughts with no bodily posture
That proskynēsis never included kissing in Greek usage
Salutation and strong love toward what is reverenced
That proskynēsis is always identical to latria toward God
questions.answer: Salutation and strong love toward what is reverenced

questions.question: The speaker uses "particularist ecclesiology" to mean what?
options: Salvation can be found across many visible communions equally
Salvation is only within the external boundaries of one specific denomination or church body
The church is purely invisible with no visible institutions
Ecumenical councils have no authority for Christians today
questions.answer: Salvation is only within the external boundaries of one specific denomination or church body

questions.question: Walther is quoted to reject making salvation depend on membership in which kind of church?
options: The visible "orthodox" (lowercase in the thesis) church and perfect agreement on every article of faith
Any congregation that sings hymns
Any church that practices infant baptism
Any church that uses the vernacular in worship
questions.answer: The visible "orthodox" (lowercase in the thesis) church and perfect agreement on every article of faith

questions.question: What does the lecture say happens to those who undertake to debate icon veneration further while wrongly disposed toward the images?
options: They are praised for academic freedom
They are anathematized by the council's language cited
They are automatically received as catechumens
They are only asked to abstain from communion for one week
questions.answer: They are anathematized by the council's language cited

questions.question: The speaker infers from the council's definition that normal human beings, to avoid anathema on the council's terms, must be able to produce what toward the physical icon?
options: Indifference, as long as they do not destroy images
Strong affection, not only external gestures
A written theological essay defending every anathema verbatim
Permission from a civil magistrate
questions.answer: Strong affection, not only external gestures

questions.question: Revelation 22:8-9 is used in the lecture to reinforce what pattern?
options: That angels require proskynēsis for salvation
That John never worshiped anyone in heaven
That proskynēsis toward an angel is repeated and forbidden a second time
That Peter and John disagreed about worship
questions.answer: That proskynēsis toward an angel is repeated and forbidden a second time

questions.question: The lecture argues that tying salvation to venerating saints' and angels' images creates tension with Scripture because those very persons did what?
options: Commanded Christians to paint their portraits for churches
Refused and forbade proskynēsis toward themselves in person
Taught that only Mary may be venerated
Equated all religious art with idols
questions.answer: Refused and forbade proskynēsis toward themselves in person
